focus: Maintainability and traceability depth for PR 5 metrics, PR 4 compatibility aliases, PR 6/7 regression tests, prose drift, and R2 adversarial validation

title: PR-4 T-027 compatibility alias surface is not actually centralized in trust-state.ts
reproduce: Run rg -n "\b(GraphFreshness|StructuralReadiness|TrustState|SkillGraphTrustState)\b" across the MCP server, scripts, hooks, and runtime config folders; trust-state.ts only exposes TrustState while GraphFreshness and StructuralReadiness remain code-graph exports.
fix: Either implement the promised one-release V1-V5 alias surface in trust-state.ts and migrate external imports toward it, or amend T-027/plan evidence to state the old code-graph type exports intentionally remain owned there.

title: PR-7 settings parity can pass by skipping every assertion in the default non-Claude test environment
reproduce: Unset CLAUDE_CODE and CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, then run the settings-driven parity suite; describe.skipIf skips before the production .claude/settings.local.json assertions run.
fix: Split runtime-independent JSON shape assertions into an always-on test and leave only true Claude interpreter behavior behind the Claude runtime guard.

title: Spec close-out summary still says the packet is planned and research-only
reproduce: Open implementation-summary.md and compare the planned/not-started metadata and body with the active implementation review state log for iterations 1-3.
fix: Update the implementation summary after Phase 5 to describe the implemented PR set, current review verdict, outstanding findings, validation evidence, and next remediation step.

title: Copilot hook README still documents the deleted mixed .claude/settings.local.json top-level bash wrapper shape
reproduce: Compare hooks/copilot/README.md:27-64 with the current .claude/settings.local.json nested-only Claude hooks block.
fix: Rewrite the Copilot registration section to describe the current Copilot-supported surface and avoid prescribing top-level bash inside .claude/settings.local.json.

evidence: .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill-advisor/tests/legacy/advisor-corpus-parity.vitest.ts:27 still points at the old corpus path; the old path is missing and the suite is included by vitest.config.ts:20.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill-advisor/bench/code-graph-query-latency.bench.ts:153 catches all errors and :164 accepts skipped reports, so invariant failures can still pass as skips.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code-graph/lib/code-graph-context.ts:176 still maps every non-fresh/non-stale/non-unknown value, including recent, to absent.
